rationale: This page provides comprehensive technical details on solar energy technologies, agriphotovoltaics, and the National Green Hydrogen Mission. It includes specific policy details for the Green Hydrogen Certification Scheme and a detailed table classifying different types of hydrogen based on production process and source.
cleanedText:
• It uses photovoltaic cells, typically made of silicon, assembled into panels, which can be used in both small-scale installations, like on rooftops, or in large solar power plants.
• It produces direct current (DC), which is then converted to alternating current (AC) by an inverter.
• Other types of technologies for harnessing Solar Power:
o Solar Thermal Energy: Harnesses the sun’s heat through solar thermal panels to generate electricity or provide heating solutions.
o Concentrated Solar Power (CSP): Harness the sun’s energy by focusing sunlight using mirrors, lenses, and tracking devices.
Related News: Agriphotovoltaics (APVs) or Solar Farming
• APVs could provide a model that maximises land-use efficiency and augments farmers’ income.
• Definition: Combined use of same land area for agricultural production and electricity production utilizing a solar PV system.
• Components: Solar panels, solar tracking systems (follow the sun for maximum efficiency), and shade-tolerant crops (protection against strong solar irradiation, winds).
4.3.4. NATIONAL GREEN HYDROGEN MISSION (NGHM)
Why in the News?
Centre launched Green Hydrogen Certification Scheme of India (GHCI) under NGHM.
About GHCI
• Ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
• Purpose: Develop detailed methodology for measurement, monitoring, reporting, onsite verification, and certification of Green Hydrogen and its derivatives.
• Nodal Authority: Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE).
• Certification details:
o Certification Scope: Shall operate at project level for green hydrogen production.
o Processes like transport and storage of hydrogen outside plant boundaries, conversion into hydrogen carriers, are excluded from certification scope.
o Two Types: concept (voluntary) and facility level certificate (mandatory).
o Calculation of GHG emissions intensity for GH production shall follow MNRE Green Hydrogen Emission Calculation Methodology.
o ‘Green’ Certification is conditional on meeting average emission intensity requirements of 2 kg CO2eq/kg H2 or less.
o Certificate is non-transferable and cannot be claimed for any emission reduction credits.
About Green Hydrogen
• Hydrogen produced through electrolysis, splitting water molecules into hydrogen and oxygen using electricity from renewable sources like solar, wind etc.
• Applications: Decarbonizing sectors like Steel Production, Refineries and fertilizer plants, transportation, energy storage, etc.
• Challenges in adoption: High Cost of producing Green Hydrogen, requires significant infrastructure.
Types of Hydrogen
| Colour | Type | Process | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Black/Brown | Black/Brown Hydrogen | Coal Gasification | Coal |
| Grey | Grey Hydrogen | Methane Reformation | Natural Gas |
| Blue | Blue Hydrogen | Coal Gasification & Methane Reformation with CCUS | Fossil Fuel |
| Turquoise | Turquoise Hydrogen | Pyrolysis | Methane |
| Pink | Pink Hydrogen | Electrolysis | Nuclear Energy |
| Green | Green Hydrogen | Electrolysis / Biomass Gasification | Renewable Energy |
